title: Lightspeed Retail Documentation
prompt_source: Inline docs prompt; CONNECTOR_DOCUMENTATION_GUIDELINES fetched via getDocGuidelines()

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
**Lightspeed Retail (R-Series POS)** is a cloud point-of-sale platform exposing a REST API under `https://api.lightspeedapp.com/API/V3/Account/{account_id}/`. This connector — `LightspeedConnector` (`CONNECTOR_TYPE = "lightspeed"`, `AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`) — wraps the operational surfaces a Shielva tenant typically needs from a Lightspeed Retail account:

| Surface | Base path | Capability |
|---|---|---|
| Account | `/Account.json` | Health/identity probe |
| Customers | `/Customer.json` | List, get, create with Contact sub-doc |
| Items | `/Item.json` | List, get, create + Prices envelope, update |
| Sales | `/Sale.json` | List, get, create + date/customer filters |
| Inventory | `/ItemShop.json` | Per-shop stock levels |
| Categories | `/Category.json` | Item category tree |
| Vendors | `/Vendor.json` | Supplier registry |
| Employees | `/Employee.json` | Staff registry |
| Shops | `/Shop.json` | Store locations / registers |

The connector normalises items + sales into `NormalizedDocument` (id = `f"{connector_id}_<kind>_{source_id}"`, tenant-scoped), surfaces standalone `async def` methods per user-requested operation (OCP), refreshes tokens on 401 (single replay), respects Lightspeed's leaky-bucket header `X-LS-API-Bucket-Level` (proactive backoff at ≥ 90%), and never embeds raw HTTP in `connector.py` (SOC — all HTTP delegated to `client/http_client.py::LightspeedHTTPClient`).

sections.title: Quick Start
sections.content:
### 1. Register an OAuth app

1. Sign in to https://developers.lightspeedhq.com/.
2. Open **Apps → New App** for Lightspeed Retail (R-Series).
3. Set the **Redirect URI** to your Shielva callback (e.g. `https://app.shielva.ai/oauth/callback/lightspeed`).
4. Copy the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** — these are install fields.
5. Find your **Account ID** under **Settings → Account** in the Lightspeed Retail dashboard (numeric).

### 2. Install in ACP

In **ACP → Manage Connectors → Lightspeed Retail**, fill in:

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| `client_id` (text) | OAuth client ID |
| `client_secret` (secret) | OAuth client secret |
| `account_id` (text) | Numeric account ID |
| `scopes` (text, optional) | Default: `employee:all employee:register` |
| `auth_url` / `token_url` (text, optional) | Override defaults if needed |
| `redirect_uri` (text, optional) | Same value configured in the developer portal |
| `rate_limit_per_min` (number) | Soft cap, default 50 |

Click **Install**. `install()` validates `client_id` + `client_secret`, initialises the `LightspeedHTTPClient`, and returns `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)` — pending OAuth completion.

### 3. Complete OAuth

The gateway redirects the operator to Lightspeed's consent screen, receives the `?code=...` callback, and calls `authorize(auth_code)`. The connector POSTs to `/auth/oauth/token` (`grant_type=authorization_code`) and stores the resulting `TokenInfo`.

### 4. First health check

`health_check()` probes `GET /Account.json`. On 2xx → `HEALTHY + CONNECTED`. On 401 with no refresh → `DEGRADED + TOKEN_EXPIRED`. On 403 → `UNHEALTHY + INVALID_CREDENTIALS`. On 429 → `DEGRADED + CONNECTED` with structured-log warning + leaky-bucket backoff.

sections.title: Authentication
sections.content:
Lightspeed Retail uses **OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code** (`AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"`). Access tokens last ~30 min; refresh tokens are long-lived in practice (no advertised expiry).

### Credential model

- `client_id` + `client_secret` — OAuth app from the Developer Portal.
- `account_id` — numeric account ID, used as a URL segment on every API call.
- `access_token` is sent as `Authorization: Bearer <token>`.

### Lifecycle

| Phase | Behaviour |
|---|---|
| `install()` | Validates `client_id` + `client_secret`. Initialises `LightspeedHTTPClient`. Does **not** call any Lightspeed endpoint. Returns `HEALTHY + PENDING`. |
| `authorize(auth_code, state)` | `POST /auth/oauth/token` with `grant_type=authorization_code`. Stores `TokenInfo`. |
| `on_token_refresh()` | `POST /auth/oauth/token` with `grant_type=refresh_token`. Preserves the stored refresh token if the response omits a new one. |
| `health_check()` | `GET /Account.json` — minimal probe. |
| `ensure_token()` (inherited) | Auto-refreshes when the access token is expired or within 60s of expiry. |

### Header contract

Every outbound API request:

```
Authorization: Bearer <access_token>
Accept:        application/json
Content-Type:  application/json    (only on POST/PUT)
```

### Refresh-on-401

The HTTP client implements a one-shot refresh-and-replay: when any request returns 401 and a `refresh_cb` is provided, the client calls the callback (which invokes `on_token_refresh()`), stores the new token, and retries the original request exactly once. If the retry also returns 401, the `LightspeedAuthError` propagates to the caller and `health_check()` maps it to `AuthStatus.TOKEN_EXPIRED`.

sections.title: Configuration
sections.content:
### Install fields (per-tenant)

Read via `self.config.get(...)` in `connector.py::__init__` — never hardcoded.

| Key | Type | Required | Read in code | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `client_id` | string | yes | `self.client_id` | OAuth client ID |
| `client_secret` | secret | yes | `self.client_secret` | OAuth client secret |
| `account_id` | string | yes (runtime) | `self.account_id` | URL segment for every API call |
| `scopes` | string | no | `self.scopes` | Space-separated scope list |
| `auth_url` | string | no | `self.auth_url` (defaults to `https://cloud.lightspeedapp.com/oauth/authorize.php`) | Override OAuth authorize URL |
| `token_url` | string | no | `self.token_url` (defaults to `https://cloud.lightspeedapp.com/auth/oauth/token`) | Override OAuth token URL |
| `redirect_uri` | string | no | `self.config['redirect_uri']` | Callback URL |
| `rate_limit_per_min` | number | no | `self.rate_limit_per_min` (default 50) | Client-side soft cap |

### Class constants (provider-wide)

```python
CONNECTOR_TYPE = "lightspeed"
AUTH_TYPE = "oauth2_code"
REQUIRED_CONFIG_KEYS = ["client_id", "client_secret"]
REQUIRED_SCOPES = ["employee:all", "employee:register"]
_STATUS_MAP = {
    401: ("DEGRADED",  "TOKEN_EXPIRED"),
    403: ("UNHEALTHY", "INVALID_CREDENTIALS"),
    429: ("DEGRADED",  "CONNECTED"),
}
_PUBLIC_API_ROOT = "https://api.lightspeedapp.com/API/V3"
```

sections.title: API Methods
sections.content: All public async methods on `LightspeedConnector`. SOC: connector.py orchestrates only; HTTP lives in `client/http_client.py::LightspeedHTTPClient`.

children.title: Lifecycle
children.content:
### `async install() -> ConnectorStatus`
Validate `client_id` + `client_secret`; persist install fields; initialise `LightspeedHTTPClient`. Does NOT call the API.

### `async authorize(auth_code, state) -> TokenInfo`
Exchange OAuth code for access + refresh tokens. POSTs to `/auth/oauth/token` with `grant_type=authorization_code`.

### `async on_token_refresh() -> TokenInfo`
Refresh the access token. POSTs `grant_type=refresh_token`. Preserves the stored refresh token if absent in the response.

### `async health_check() -> ConnectorStatus`
Probes `GET /Account.json`. Classifies failures via `_STATUS_MAP`.

### `async sync(since=None, full=False, kb_id=None, webhook_url=None) -> SyncResult`
Iterates Lightspeed items → `NormalizedDocument` → `ingest_document`. Incremental via `timeStamp >,<checkpoint>`. Returns `SyncResult` with `documents_found/_synced/_failed`.

children.title: Customers
children.content:
### `async list_customers(limit=100, offset=0, search=None) -> Dict`
`GET /Customer.json`. `search` becomes a `lastName=~,%<term>%` wildcard filter.

### `async get_customer(customer_id) -> Dict`
`GET /Customer/{customer_id}.json`.

### `async create_customer(first_name, last_name, email=None, phone=None, contact=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Customer.json`. If `contact` is supplied it is sent verbatim; otherwise `email`/`phone` are packed into `{Contact: {Emails: {ContactEmail: [...]}, Phones: {ContactPhone: [...]}}}`.

children.title: Items
children.content:
### `async list_items(limit=100, offset=0, search=None, category_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /Item.json`. `search` → `description=~,%<term>%` wildcard. `category_id` → `categoryID=<id>` exact filter.

### `async get_item(item_id, load_relations=None) -> Dict`
`GET /Item/{item_id}.json`. Pass `load_relations` (JSON-encoded list) to hydrate sub-entities.

### `async create_item(description, default_cost, default_price, item_type="default", category_id=None, manufacturer_id=None, tax_id=None) -> Dict`
`POST /Item.json`. Builds the `Prices.ItemPrice` envelope with both `Default` and `MSRP` rows from `default_price`.

### `async update_item(item_id, fields) -> Dict`
`PUT /Item/{item_id}.json`. Sends `fields` as the partial update body.

children.title: Sales
children.content:
### `async list_sales(limit=100, offset=0, completed=None, start_date=None, end_date=None, customer_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /Sale.json`. Date filters use Lightspeed's interval operators:

- `start_date` + `end_date` → `createTime=><,start,end` (between)
- `start_date` only → `createTime=>,start` (after)
- `end_date` only → `createTime=<,end` (before)

### `async get_sale(sale_id, load_relations=None) -> Dict`
`GET /Sale/{sale_id}.json`.

### `async create_sale(sale) -> Dict`
`POST /Sale.json`. `sale` is the raw Lightspeed Sale body, e.g. `{"completed": "true", "shopID": "1", "registerID": "1", "employeeID": "7", "SaleLines": {"SaleLine": [{"unitPrice": "9.99", "unitQuantity": "1", "itemID": "1234"}]}}`.

children.title: Inventory · Categories · Vendors · Employees · Shops
children.content:
### `async list_inventory(item_id=None, shop_id=None) -> Dict`
`GET /ItemShop.json` with optional `itemID` / `shopID` filters.

### `async list_categories(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Category.json`.

### `async list_vendors(limit=100, offset=0) -> Dict`
`GET /Vendor.json`.

### `async list_employees() -> Dict`
`GET /Employee.json`.

### `async list_shops() -> Dict`
`GET /Shop.json`.

sections.title: Error Handling
sections.content:
Exception hierarchy in `exceptions.py`:

```
LightspeedError                       # base; carries status_code + response_body
├── LightspeedAuthError                # 401 / 403
├── LightspeedBadRequestError          # 400
├── LightspeedNotFound                 # 404
├── LightspeedConflictError            # 409
├── LightspeedRateLimitError           # 429 — retry_after_s
└── LightspeedServerError              # 5xx
```

Back-compat aliases preserved:
```
LightspeedNetworkError    = LightspeedServerError
LightspeedNotFoundError   = LightspeedNotFound
```

### Retry behaviour (`client/http_client.py::_request`)

| Status | Action |
|---|---|
| 400 / 404 / 409 | Raise immediately |
| 401 | One-shot refresh-and-replay if `refresh_cb` is provided; else raise `LightspeedAuthError` |
| 403 | Raise `LightspeedAuthError` |
| 429 | Honour `Retry-After` (fall back to exponential backoff), retry up to 4 |
| 5xx | Exponential backoff `_RETRY_DELAY_S * _BACKOFF_FACTOR^attempt + jitter`, retry up to 4 |
| `httpx.HTTPError` | Backoff, retry up to 4, then raise `LightspeedNetworkError` |

### Leaky-bucket throttle

Lightspeed returns `X-LS-API-Bucket-Level: "current/max"`. The client parses this and sleeps **1 s** before the *next* call whenever the bucket is ≥ 90 % full — a proactive guard that avoids 429s during sustained read traffic.

### How errors surface to the gateway

`health_check()` catches exceptions and maps via `_STATUS_MAP`:

```
401 (no recovery) → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  TOKEN_EXPIRED)
403               → ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)
429               → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
RefreshError      → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   EXPIRED)
NetworkError      → ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE,   CONNECTED)
other             → ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED,  CONNECTED)
```

sections.title: Troubleshooting
sections.content:
| Symptom | Probable cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|
| `ConnectorStatus(OFFLINE, MISSING_CREDENTIALS)` at install | `client_id` or `client_secret` blank | Re-enter both from the Lightspeed Developer Portal. |
| `ConnectorStatus(HEALTHY, PENDING)` after install but UI says "not connected" | OAuth not yet completed | Click **Connect** to start the OAuth flow; the gateway will call `authorize(auth_code)` on callback. |
| `ConnectorStatus(UNHEALTHY, INVALID_CREDENTIALS)` at health_check | 403 from Lightspeed — token lacks scope | Re-authorise with the right `scopes` (e.g. `employee:all`). |
| `ConnectorStatus(DEGRADED, TOKEN_EXPIRED)` at health_check | 401 — refresh failed (refresh token revoked or app uninstalled in Lightspeed) | Re-run the OAuth flow to mint a fresh token pair. |
| `lightspeed.bucket_high` warnings spike | Bucket ≥ 90 % full → proactive 1 s backoff | Lower `rate_limit_per_min`, batch fewer requests, or pace your callers. |
| `lightspeed.transient_status status=429` warnings spike | Hitting the 429 ceiling | The client retries with `Retry-After` (exponential fallback). Investigate caller patterns; consider splitting heavy syncs across windows. |
| 404 on `get_item(N)` | Wrong `item_id`, item deleted, or wrong account | Verify the ID exists; confirm `account_id` matches the account that owns the item. |
| `ImportError: cannot import name 'LightspeedNetworkError'` | Older `exceptions.py` without back-compat aliases | Already fixed — `LightspeedNetworkError = LightspeedServerError` alias added in `exceptions.py`. |
| Sync stalls / re-syncs the same items | `set_metadata('last_sync_at', ...)` never persisted | Verify the `BaseConnector` metadata sink (Redis/Mongo) is reachable; check structlog for `lightspeed.sync.failed` errors. |
